business_ideas.idea: Entertainment-first minor league baseball team with flat-rate fan pricing and showmanship

predictions.text: Savannah Bananas will sustain long-term like Harlem Globetrotters touring format, not one-time phenomenon

dynamics: Shaan narrates Jesse Cole's entrepreneurial origin story with deep analysis; Sam contextualizes with frameworks (Steve Jobs insanely great, unreasonable hospitality, Monster Jam comparisons). Unified high energy and genuine awe throughout. Episodic tangents but focused enthusiasm.
frameworks: Insanely great - obsessive attention to small details nobody sees creates emotional impact
Idea muscle - generating 10+ ideas daily strengthens creativity through repetition
Unreasonable hospitality - systematically exceeding expectations creates memorable differentiation
Strategy others disagree with - true competitive advantage requires doing things competitors wouldn't
Banana Ball format - entertainment-optimized sports with custom rules prioritizing pacing and family experience
learnings: Obsessing over fan data (security footage, retention curves, seating engagement) reveals where customers disengage and drives business scale
Hire people who are down to execute unproven ideas with limited evidence, not people who critique every decision
Invest scarce resources in brand identity first (paid $12k logo while living on $40/week) to signal values and build narrative
Differentiation requires doing things competitors call inefficient or would fundamentally disagree with
Generate lots of ideas daily (10-40+) to train your brain through repetition to produce better ideas
Live experiences satisfy primal human needs that digital entertainment cannot replicate, making them AI-proof
Your team's culture amplifies or censors your fragile ideas - hire for willingness to try rather than risk-filtering

banger_quotes.quote: I literally wrote in all caps this guy deserves a billion dollars
When you get big you'll worry about that stuff. It's the other way around
It's the insane part that's the part nobody wants to do
If you just do expected norms it's table stakes, you get zero credit

numbers_dropped: 3 million person waiting list for tickets
2 million fans attended last year (up from 500K, then 1M progression)
20-30 million dollars per year in merchandise sales
200 fans per game original team (Gastonia Grizzlies)
$40 per week living expenses during building phase
notable: Episode designated 'Billy of the Week' with unusual emotional intensity - Shaan says 'I pray this makes it to them' about Jesse Cole. Hosts explicitly request help getting Jesse Cole as future guest. Ends with Spotify growth test asking listeners to comment to trigger ranking boost.
